question: [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker acting as msg.sender, under the precondition that referralFeePct is nonzero and maxBuyerAssets is set to exactly cover filledBuyerAssets (i.e., maxBuyerAssets == filledBuyerAssets), trigger the call sequence pullToken -> forceApproveMax -> take loop -> referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets), violating the invariant that msg.sender pays at most maxBuyerAssets, causing scoped impact: arithmetic underflow revert that permanently blocks the user from completing a valid fill even though all offers were successfully taken? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct in [1, WAD-1] and maxBuyerAssets == filledBuyerAssets and assert the subtraction maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets never underflows.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that referralFeePct approaches WAD-1 and filledBuyerAssets is nonzero, trigger the call sequence where referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) produces a value much larger than filledBuyerAssets, violating the invariant that total cost equals filledBuyerAssets + referralFeeAssets <= maxBuyerAssets, causing scoped impact: user's transaction reverts after all takes succeed and tokens are already pulled, wasting gas and blocking the intended operation? Proof idea: unit test with referralFeePct = WAD/2, filledBuyerAssets = 1e18, maxBuyerAssets = 1e18 and assert referralFeeAssets = 1e18 causes underflow.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that referralFeePct > 0 and targetBuyerAssets is set to the exact amount needed to fill targetFilledBuyerAssets worth of offers, trigger the call sequence pullToken(loanToken, msg.sender, targetBuyerAssets) -> referralFeeAssets = targetBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD) -> targetFilledBuyerAssets = targetBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets -> fill loop -> safeTransfer(loanToken, referralFeeRecipient, referralFeeAssets) with no return transfer to msg.sender, violating the invariant that msg.sender receives back any unspent tokens, causing scoped impact: if filledBuyerAssets < targetFilledBuyerAssets the require reverts but if rounding causes filledBuyerAssets == targetFilledBuyerAssets with fewer actual tokens pulled by Midnight, residual loan tokens are permanently stuck in the bundler? Proof idea: fuzz targetBuyerAssets and referralFeePct and assert bundler loanToken balance is zero after every successful call.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that referralFeePct > 0 and targetSellerAssets is set such that targetFilledSellerAssets = targetSellerAssets + targetSellerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct), trigger the call sequence where the take loop accumulates filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets but the bundler sends only referralFeeAssets + targetSellerAssets, violating the invariant that all tokens received by the bundler from Midnight are forwarded to recipients, causing scoped impact: if actual resSellerAssets from any take exceeds the per-iteration target, excess loan tokens are permanently stuck in the bundler? Proof idea: fuzz sellerPrice, referralFeePct, and units per take and assert bundler loanToken balance is zero after every successful call.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that the bundler holds a nonzero residual loanToken balance from a prior incomplete or reverted operation, trigger the call sequence supplyCollateral -> take loop with address(this) as receiverIfTakerIsSeller -> safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets), violating the invariant that the receiver receives only tokens earned in the current call, causing scoped impact: receiver receives more loan tokens than earned in the current transaction, draining residual tokens left by a prior user? Proof idea: state test where bundler.loanToken.balanceOf(bundler) > 0 before call and assert receiver receives exactly filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that the bundler holds a nonzero residual loanToken balance from a prior incomplete operation, trigger the call sequence supplyCollateral -> take loop with address(this) as receiverIfTakerIsSeller -> safeTransfer(loanToken, referralFeeRecipient, referralFeeAssets) -> safeTransfer(loanToken, receiver, targetSellerAssets), violating the invariant that only tokens earned in the current call are distributed, causing scoped impact: referralFeeRecipient or receiver receives more than their entitlement, draining residual tokens from prior users? Proof idea: state test where bundler.loanToken.balanceOf(bundler) > 0 before call and assert total outflow equals filledSellerAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that onBehalf's debt is less than units = assets - assets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD), trigger the call sequence pullToken(loanToken, msg.sender, assets) -> forceApproveMax -> IMidnight.repay(market, units, onBehalf) where Midnight subtracts units from position.debt causing underflow revert, violating the invariant that a user can repay any amount up to their debt via the bundler, causing scoped impact: user's transaction reverts after tokens are pulled, blocking a valid repay-and-withdraw flow? Proof idea: unit test with debt < units and assert the call reverts with CastOverflow or arithmetic underflow.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that referralFeePct > 0 and assets is set to exactly repay onBehalf's full debt D (i.e., assets = D * WAD / (WAD - referralFeePct) rounded down), trigger the call sequence where units = assets - assets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD) is strictly less than D due to mulDivDown rounding, violating the invariant that passing the documented formula assets = floor(D * WAD / (WAD - pct)) fully repays debt D, causing scoped impact: user pays full assets but debt is not fully cleared, leaving a dust debt that blocks collateral withdrawal? Proof idea: fuzz D and referralFeePct and assert units == D when assets = floor(D * WAD / (WAD - referralFeePct)).

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that referralFeePct == 0 and maxBuyerAssets > filledBuyerAssets, trigger the call sequence where the return transfer safeTransfer(loanToken, msg.sender, maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - 0) succeeds but the bundler retains no tokens, violating the invariant that the bundler holds zero loanToken balance after every successful call, causing scoped impact: no fund loss in isolation but confirms the zero-referral-fee path is safe and the non-zero path is the only underflow risk? Proof idea: invariant test asserting bundler.loanToken.balanceOf(bundler) == 0 after every successful buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral call.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender observe that the referral fee formula in buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral is referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct) while in buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral it is referralFeeAssets = targetBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD), under the precondition that both functions are called with the same referralFeePct and equivalent fill amounts, violating the invariant that equivalent operations charge equivalent fees, causing scoped impact: user pays a different effective fee depending on which function they call, leading to unexpected token loss? Proof idea: differential test with same referralFeePct and fill amount across both functions and assert fee amounts are consistent.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker observe that the referral fee formula in supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget is referralFeeAssets = filledSellerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD) while in supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget it is referralFeeAssets = targetSellerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct), under the precondition that both functions are called with the same referralFeePct and equivalent fill amounts, violating the invariant that equivalent sell operations charge equivalent fees, causing scoped impact: taker receives a different net amount depending on which function they call, leading to unexpected token loss? Proof idea: differential test with same referralFeePct and fill amount across both functions and assert net receiver amounts are consistent.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that takes[] contains a single offer with unitsToTake == targetUnits and the offer's actual buyerAssets returned by Midnight.take is larger than expected due to mulDivUp rounding (offer.buy == false path: buyerAssets = units.mulDivUp(buyerPrice, WAD)), trigger the call sequence where filledBuyerAssets > expected and maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets underflows, violating the invariant that the bundler never spends more than maxBuyerAssets, causing scoped impact: transaction reverts after tokens are pulled, blocking the user's intended operation? Proof idea: fuzz tick and units near rounding boundaries and assert filledBuyerAssets + referralFeeAssets <= maxBuyerAssets always holds.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits returns units U such that Midnight.take returns resBuyerAssets > targetFilledBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets due to rounding divergence between the view computation and the actual take execution, trigger the call sequence where filledBuyerAssets overshoots targetFilledBuyerAssets, violating the invariant that require(filledBuyerAssets == targetFilledBuyerAssets) is always satisfiable, causing scoped impact: user's transaction always reverts for certain tick/fee combinations, permanently blocking the assets-target buy flow? Proof idea: fuzz tick, settlementFee, and targetBuyerAssets and assert buyerAssetsToUnits(U) followed by take(U) always yields resBuyerAssets <= targetFilledBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that TakeAmountsLib.sellerAssetsToUnits returns units U such that Midnight.take returns resSellerAssets > targetFilledSellerAssets - filledSellerAssets due to rounding divergence between the view computation and the actual take execution, trigger the call sequence where filledSellerAssets overshoots targetFilledSellerAssets, violating the invariant that require(filledSellerAssets == targetFilledSellerAssets) is always satisfiable, causing scoped impact: user's transaction always reverts for certain tick/fee combinations, permanently blocking the assets-target sell flow? Proof idea: fuzz tick, settlementFee, and targetSellerAssets and assert sellerAssetsToUnits(U) followed by take(U) always yields resSellerAssets <= targetFilledSellerAssets - filledSellerAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that the settlement fee changes between the touchMarket call (line 64) and the take calls in the loop, trigger the call sequence touchMarket -> feeSetter.setMarketSettlementFee -> take loop where buyerPrice differs from what was expected at touchMarket time, violating the invariant that the user pays the price they observed at touchMarket, causing scoped impact: user pays more buyerAssets than expected, and if filledBuyerAssets + referralFeeAssets > maxBuyerAssets the transaction reverts, blocking the operation? Proof idea: state test where feeSetter changes settlementFee between touchMarket and take and assert filledBuyerAssets matches the post-change fee.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that collateralSupplies[] is nonempty and all takes[] fail (caught by try/catch), trigger the call sequence supplyCollateral (modifies Midnight state) -> take loop (all revert, caught) -> require(filledUnits == targetUnits) reverts, violating the invariant that a revert undoes all state changes including the collateral supply, causing scoped impact: if the revert correctly undoes supplyCollateral, no fund loss occurs, but if any external state is not reverted (e.g., Permit2 nonce consumed), the user loses a permit? Proof idea: state test where all takes revert and assert Midnight.collateral(id, taker, index) is unchanged after the bundler call reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that a Permit2 signature is used for collateral supply (CollateralSupply.permit.kind == PermitKind.Permit2) and the bundler call reverts after consuming the Permit2 nonce, trigger the call sequence IPermit2.permitTransferFrom (nonce consumed) -> supplyCollateral -> take loop fails -> revert, violating the invariant that a reverted bundler call leaves the user's Permit2 nonce unconsumed, causing scoped impact: user's Permit2 nonce is permanently consumed even though no collateral was supplied, forcing the user to sign a new permit? Proof idea: state test where all takes revert and assert Permit2 nonce is not consumed after the bundler call reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that loanTokenPermit.kind == PermitKind.ERC2612 and a front-runner consumes the ERC2612 permit before the bundler call, trigger the call sequence try IERC20Permit.permit (reverts, caught) -> safeTransferFrom(loanToken, msg.sender, address(this), maxBuyerAssets) where msg.sender has not pre-approved the bundler, violating the invariant that the bundler can always pull tokens when a valid permit is provided, causing scoped impact: user's transaction reverts at safeTransferFrom, blocking the intended buy operation? Proof idea: state test where permit is front-run and msg.sender has zero allowance to bundler and assert the call reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that takes[] is empty (takes.length == 0), trigger the call sequence where takes[0].offer.market.loanToken is accessed at line 62 causing an array out-of-bounds revert before any tokens are pulled, violating the invariant that the bundler provides a meaningful error for empty takes[], causing scoped impact: user's transaction reverts with a generic panic rather than a descriptive error, blocking the operation with no clear diagnostic? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that collateralWithdrawals[] is nonempty but takes[] is empty, trigger the call sequence where takes[0] access reverts before any collateral withdrawal is attempted, violating the invariant that collateral withdrawals are processed when takes[] is empty, causing scoped impact: user cannot withdraw collateral via the bundler without providing at least one take, blocking a valid collateral-only withdrawal flow? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: forceApproveMax] Can an unprivileged attacker, under the precondition that the bundler holds a nonzero loanToken balance (residual from a prior reverted or partial operation) and forceApproveMax has already set allowance to type(uint256).max for MIDNIGHT, trigger the call sequence where Midnight.take pulls buyerAssets from the bundler including the residual balance, violating the invariant that Midnight only pulls tokens belonging to the current msg.sender, causing scoped impact: residual tokens from a prior user are drained by Midnight on behalf of the current caller? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that taker != msg.sender and IMidnight.isAuthorized(taker, msg.sender) is true, trigger the call sequence where msg.sender specifies an arbitrary receiver address to receive filledSellerAssets - referralFeeAssets, violating the invariant that the taker controls where their loan tokens are sent, causing scoped impact: an authorized operator redirects the taker's loan proceeds to an attacker-controlled address? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that taker != msg.sender and IMidnight.isAuthorized(taker, msg.sender) is true, trigger the call sequence where msg.sender specifies an arbitrary collateralReceiver address for withdrawCollateral, violating the invariant that the taker controls where their collateral is sent, causing scoped impact: an authorized operator redirects the taker's collateral to an attacker-controlled address? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that onBehalf != msg.sender and IMidnight.isAuthorized(onBehalf, msg.sender) is true, trigger the call sequence where msg.sender specifies an arbitrary collateralReceiver address for withdrawCollateral, violating the invariant that onBehalf controls where their collateral is sent, causing scoped impact: an authorized operator redirects onBehalf's collateral to an attacker-controlled address after repaying their debt? [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: Medium Periphery integration bug that can cause user loss when interacting with the core Midnight contract as intended'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged msg.sender, under the precondition that takes[] contains offers from different markets (bypassing the InconsistentMarket check by crafting offers with the same toId but different market structs), trigger the call sequence where collateralWithdrawals use takes[0].offer.market but the actual market for later takes differs, violating the invariant that all takes and collateral withdrawals operate on the same market, causing scoped impact: collateral is withdrawn from the wrong market or the wrong collateral index?
